title: parietaria pensylvanica var. obtusa
intro: Parietaria pensylvanica var. obtusa, also known as a variety of the Pilea plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae. It is a plant traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipruritic properties. The health benefits of Parietaria pensylvanica var. obtusa include relief from skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis, as well as its use in treating digestive issues and fever reduction. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as an infusion or tincture for medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.
